what_it_does: Defines a systemd timer that activates company-routine-self_status.service every day at 09:00:00, with persistence enabled and membership in company.target.
observations: Persistent=true ensures a missed trigger runs after boot.
Unit=company-routine-self_status.service names the service to activate.
Install section uses WantedBy=company.target instead of timers.target.
An inline comment explains the company.target binding is for managed lifecycle and visibility in `company status`.
Description references an S-R9.1 schedule arm.
The file lives under a generated/ path.
standouts: Bound to company.target rather than timers.target.
Generated artifact with inline rationale for target choice.
conventions: Follows standard systemd unit file sections [Unit], [Timer], [Install].
Timer filename matches the service it triggers.
Includes descriptive metadata and rationale comments.
Uses a generated directory for derived unit files.
concerns: Generated file; the source template or generator is not shown here.
No local guarantee that company.target or the referenced service exists.
Persistent=true with a daily timer could queue multiple runs after extended downtime.
notes: A generated daily 09:00 systemd timer that ties its lifecycle to company.target and activates a self_status routine service.
questions: What is the S-R9.1 schedule arm and where is it defined?
Which generator or template produced this file?
Is company.target guaranteed to exist wherever this timer is installed?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose of running the self_status routine daily at 09:00 and binding it to company.target matches the timer configuration.
apparent_themes: Managed lifecycle over bare systemd defaults.
Operational observability and explicit coupling to a company-wide target.
intention_signals: The file intends to make a scheduled self-status check a first-class, company-managed service member rather than an orphaned timer, ensuring it runs reliably and appears in company status.
novelty: The deliberate rejection of timers.target in favor of company.target to satisfy a requirement for visible managed membership.
contribution: It contributes a daily, persistent, company-target-bound timer definition that activates a self_status service, making the schedule and lifecycle relationship explicit.
summary_for_embedding: A generated systemd timer unit that activates company-routine-self_status.service daily at 09:00 with Persistent=true and is installed into company.target.
intention_for_embedding: To schedule the self_status routine as a managed member of the company target so it runs reliably and remains visible in company status.
